scope.summary: Ruby Thread/Fiber runtime calls now reuse shared task scheduling and lifecycle obligations.

capability_alignment.capability: map proof-backed Ruby concurrency runtime calls onto existing task-spawn scheduling and handle-lifecycle obligations
not_a_feature_list: No new semantic kernel API was added.
No exact admission path was opened for Ruby Thread/Fiber calls.
Ruby support reuses shared task-spawn, task-handle, and concurrency scheduling labels instead of adding selector-specific obligations.
Same-file Thread/Fiber root definitions keep reporting closed.

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The runtime root must be the unshadowed Ruby Thread or Fiber constant within the same file.

assertions: Thread.new/start/fork report shared task-spawn, task-handle, and cancellation/liveness obligations
Fiber.new/schedule report shared task-spawn, task-handle, and cancellation/liveness obligations
Ruby Thread/Fiber rows appear under task-spawn scheduling in recall-loss reports
Thread constant assignment blocks Thread.new/start/fork attribution
Fiber constant assignment blocks Fiber.new/schedule attribution
Thread.current does not report spawn or lifecycle obligations

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The pricing script now treats Ruby Thread/Fiber calls as reporting-supported closed task-spawn boundaries and includes Thread.start.

real_corpus_spot_checks.command: cargo run -q -p nose-cli -- verify bench/repos/puma/lib/puma/server.rb bench/repos/puma/lib/puma/thread_pool.rb bench/repos/rack/test/spec_multipart.rb bench/repos/asciidoctor/test/test_helper.rb --max-violations 0 --recall-loss-report target/recall-loss.ruby-thread-fiber-runtime.spot.json

validation_commands: cargo run -q -p nose-cli -- verify bench/repos/puma/lib/puma/server.rb bench/repos/puma/lib/puma/thread_pool.rb bench/repos/rack/test/spec_multipart.rb bench/repos/asciidoctor/test/test_helper.rb --max-violations 0 --recall-loss-report target/recall-loss.ruby-thread-fiber-runtime.spot.json

release_verify_timing.baseline_reference: origin/main release verify crates warm real_seconds = 1.59
release_verify_timing.assessment: Paired local release timing did not show a large regression; current warm timing was below the origin/main warm timing in this run.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: Ruby Thread/Fiber calls are now attributed precisely enough for recall-loss reporting, but exact recovery remains closed until scheduling, lifecycle, callback effect, exception propagation, and thread/fiber identity contracts are dependency-closed.
